what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter attachment record that registers an image asset (target image://design-source/capital-raise/p-19) under the design-source channel with an attachment type of images and a timestamp.
observations: The entire content is YAML frontmatter with no body text
target uses an image:// URI pointing to design-source/capital-raise/p-19
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone
id follows an att- prefix pattern
standouts: References an image at capital-raise/p-19 via a custom image:// URI
Typed as an images attachment in the design-source channel
Contains only metadata, no visual or descriptive payload
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata
Target reference uses a URI scheme rather than a filesystem path
concerns: No alt text, caption, or description of the image
No explicit author or uploader field
Target URI cannot be resolved or validated from the file alone
notes: A minimal image-attachment ledger entry that points to a capital-raise design asset in the design-source channel.
questions: What is the entity or document p-19 in capital-raise?
What image format and dimensions does the target have?
Who added this attachment and what channel entry does it relate to?
Is image:// a resolvable scheme in this system?
intention_signals: It appears to catalog an image from the design-source channel so the capital-raise/p-19 visual asset can be located, referenced, and audited as part of design history.
novelty: The use of an image:// URI scheme as a target reference in an otherwise conventional YAML attachment record.
contribution: Provides a typed, timestamped pointer from a channel attachment record to an image asset, supporting retrieval and audit linkage for design-source media.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record of type images that targets image://design-source/capital-raise/p-19 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To register an image asset from the design-source channel for reference within a capital-raise design artifact.
